title: melilotus albus var. argutus
intro: Melilotus albus var. argutus, also known as sweet clover, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce coumarin, a natural anticoagulant that has been used to treat blood-related disorders and prevent blood clots. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Sweet clover can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs like valerian root to enhance its sedative effects.
